answers: Hydroponics is a method of growing plants without soil, using mineral nutrient solutions in water. The roots grow directly in the nutrient-rich water or in inert growing media.
Hydroponics holo mati chara shudhu pani ar nutrients diye gach folanoo. DWC, NFT, drip system — different hydroponic methods ache.
Hydroponic systems include Deep Water Culture (DWC), Nutrient Film Technique (NFT), and drip systems. They use 90% less water than soil farming and grow plants faster.
To start hydroponics at home, you can begin with a simple DWC system — a container with nutrient solution, an air pump, and net pots. Lettuce and herbs are easiest to start with.
